summary: Scan skills at `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` so one project-root harness_path captures skills and rules together — drop the repo-root `skills/`/bare-dir slice-1 leftover

edit.description: Change `discover_skill_dirs` to scan `<harness>/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` — the skill kind's real Claude Code read locus (specs/15-kinds.md, the adapter's read face: a skill loads from `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` or not at all). Drop the bare-`<harness>/SKILL.md` and repo-root `<harness>/skills/` scans (slice-1 fixture leftover / plugin layout — a repo-root `skills/` tree is the plugin kind's business, never the project convention, per specs/20-surface.md). Correct the module header's 'root asymmetry' note (lines ~3-6, ~34-37) and the `discover_rule_files` doc's 'asymmetry' line. Move the internal `write_fixture_harness` skills under `.claude/skills/`; drop/retarget `imports_a_bare_harness_that_is_itself_a_skill` and `skips_non_skill_dirs_and_files`; add a test that one `run(project_root, into)` captures both skills (`.claude/skills/`) and rules (`.claude/rules/`) together. This entry owns the scan-locus change.

Correct the module doc's discovery note (line ~23) and move the drift tests' harness skills (lines ~1203/1240/1275) from `<harness>/skills/coordinate/` under `<harness>/.claude/skills/coordinate/` so the re-scan through `discover_skill_dirs` still finds them.
`install`'s harness-scan tests build skills at `<root>/skills/`; move them under `<root>/.claude/skills/` so `discover_skill_dirs` (used at lines ~334/342) discovers them at the corrected locus.

Correct the `SessionStart.harness_path` doc comment (line ~167) that still names 'a skills/* tree, a bare skill dir' — the one-shot import/session-start path inherits the new locus automatically, so the doc must describe `.claude/skills/` + `.claude/rules/`.
Move the harness fixture's skill(s) under `.claude/skills/`; regenerate the `acceptance__coordinate_import_surface` snapshot whose `source_path` now carries the `.claude/skills/` prefix.
Move the import-CLI harness fixtures (lines ~83/480/484/549) from `<root>/skills/` to `<root>/.claude/skills/`.
Move the apply harness fixtures (lines ~69/76/186) from `harness/skills/coordinate` to `harness/.claude/skills/coordinate` (workspace-side `.temper/skills/` stays).
Move the re-add harness fixtures (lines ~65/69/80/383) from `harness/skills/` to `harness/.claude/skills/`.
Move the harness skill fixture (line ~64) under `.claude/skills/`; regenerate the `bundle__bundle_is_deterministic` snapshot if its recorded source_path shifts.
Move the session-start harness fixture (line ~63) from `root/skills/` to `root/.claude/skills/`.
Move the harness-scan skill fixture (line ~61) under `.claude/skills/`; the `.temper/skills/` surface fixture (line ~81) is unaffected.
Move `import_harness`'s skill fixture (line ~81) from `harness/skills/` to `harness/.claude/skills/`.
Move `write_harness`'s skill fixture (line ~183) under `.claude/skills/`.
Move the harness-scan skill fixture (line ~68) under `.claude/skills/`; the `.temper/skills/` surface fixture (line ~88) is unaffected.
Move the harness-scan skill fixture (line ~87) under `.claude/skills/`.
Move the install harness fixtures (lines ~79/165) from `root/skills/` to `root/.claude/skills/`.

If any harness fixture writes skills at repo-root `skills/`, move it under `.claude/skills/`; regenerate the `read_verbs__*` snapshots if a recorded source_path shifts (the `.temper/skills/` surface fixtures are unaffected).

tests.asserts: one `run(project_root, into)` over a standard project (`.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` + `.claude/rules/*.md`) captures skills AND rules together; skills are discovered under `.claude/skills/`, and a repo-root `skills/` tree is no longer scanned by the skill kind.

acceptance: `temper import <project-root>` from a standard Claude Code project captures both `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` and `.claude/rules/*.md` in one pass; the repo-root `skills/` tree and bare-skill-dir are no longer scanned by the skill kind; session-start and `check --harness` inherit the locus; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green with snapshots regenerated.
notes: Inbox BUG (first external-harness contact): scan roots were asymmetric — skills at repo-root `skills/`, rules at `.claude/rules/` — so no single harness_path caught a standard project. Fix per the locus doctrine (specs/15-kinds.md read face): the skill kind reads `.claude/skills/`; a repo-root `skills/` tree is the plugin kind's business. Wide test ripple from one scan-fn change, but one atomic entry — no parallel conflict. AGENT-KIND also lists import.rs but is deferred.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` imported as one AGENT.md member document, checked by an authored agent package

gate.reason: the requirements/satisfies reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; the whole surface-language chain it waited on has shipped, so the blocker is priority alone — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR — the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs`: typed YAML frontmatter on the projection side (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`), unknown keys preserved, content-faithful body, provenance. Identity is the path (file stem). The surface member is one AGENT.md document (clause-module header) like every kind.

The agent kind's authored built-in package (clauses + guidance) in the one-document medium, embedded with the std-lib (the EMBED-BUILTIN-PACKAGES model — NOT a contracts/agent.toml). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*` and a `WorkspaceError` arm; update the load tests.
Add `agent_features` mirroring `rule_features`.
Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to one `<into>/agents/<name>/AGENT.md` member document, add an `[[agent]]` lock row per agent.
Bind the agent package, wire admissibility + conformance streams and `by_kind` for the kind.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the agent package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction. Shaped to the surface-language forms — one AGENT.md member document, an authored PACKAGE.md, no meta.toml, no contracts/agent.toml. On revival its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes via blockedBy against any then-open entry touching those files.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than blindly clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.
